query:
You are working on the pinterest/ktlint repository.
The codebase is at version 0.49 (commit: 315e75d0).

## Issue to Resolve

experimental:function-return-type-spacing can violate max-line rule
<!-- The bug you're experiencing might have already be reported!
Please search in the [issues](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues) before creating one. -->

## Steps to Reproduce
<!--- Provide a code example, or an unambiguous set of steps to -->
<!--- reproduce this bug. Minimize the example to the bare -->
<!--- minimum required to reproduce the bug. Small, well written -->
<!--- example are proven to be resolved quicker. -->

```
    fun thisIsAVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryLongFunction():
        AndAlsoALongReturnType {

    }
```

will reformat to:
```
    fun thisIsAVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryVeryLongFunction(): AndAlsoALongReturnType {

    }
```

Which will violate the max-line-length rule.

The rule may need to make an exception for parameterless functions, or honor the max line length.

## Your Environment
<!--- Include as many relevant details about the environment you experienced the bug in -->
* Version of ktlint used:
* Relevant parts of the `.editorconfig` settings
```
[*.{kt,kts}]
max_line_length = 120
```
* Name and version (or code for custom task) of integration used (Gradle plugin, Maven plugin, command line, custom Gradle task): ktlint 0.48.1, CLI

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `CHANGELOG.md`
- `ktlint-ruleset-standard/src/main/kotlin/com/pinterest/ktlint/ruleset/standard/rules/FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

content.thinking:
Let me start by understanding the issue. The problem is that the `function-return-type-spacing` rule is reformatting code in a way that can violate the `max-line-length` rule. 

Specifically, when a function has its return type on a new line (to avoid exceeding max line length), the rule is moving it back to the same line, which can cause the line to exceed the max line length.

Let me first look at the relevant file to understand the current implementation.

 43→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 44→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType.PropertyValueParser.EnumValueParser
 45→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 46→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafElement
 47→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.psi.impl.source.tree.LeafPsiElement
 48→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.utils.addToStdlib.ifTrue
 49→
 50→public class FunctionSignatureRule :
 51→    StandardRule(
 52→        id = "function-signature",
 53→        visitorModifiers = setOf(
 54→            // Run after wrapping and spacing rules
 55→            VisitorModifier.RunAsLateAsPossible,
 56→        ),
 57→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(
 58→            INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 59→            INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 60→            MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 61→            FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY,
 62→            FUNCTION_BODY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_PROPERTY,
 63→        ),
 64→    ),
 65→    Rule.Experimental {
 66→    private var indent: String? = null
 67→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 68→    private var functionSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters =
 69→        FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 70→    private var functionBodyExpressionWrapping = FUNCTION_BODY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 71→
 72→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 73→        functionSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters = editorConfig[
 74→            FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY,
 75→        ]
 76→        functionBodyExpressionWrapping = editorConfig[FUNCTION_BODY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_PROPERTY]
 77→        val indentConfig = IndentConfig(
 78→            indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 79→            tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 80→        )
 81→        indent = indentConfig.indent
 82→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 83→    }
 84→
 85→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 86→        node: ASTNode,
 87→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 88→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 89→    ) {
 90→        if (node.elementType == FUN) {
 91→            node
 92→                .functionSignatureNodes()
 93→                .any { it.elementType == EOL_COMMENT || it.elementType == BLOCK_COMMENT }
 94→                .ifTrue {
 95→                    // Rewriting function signatures in a consistent manner is hard or sometimes even impossible. For
 96→                    // example a multiline signature which could fit on one line can not be rewritten in case it
 97→                    // contains an EOL comment. Rewriting a single line signature which exceeds the max line length to a
 98→                    // multiline signature is hard when it contains block comments. For now, it does not seem worth the
 99→                    // effort to attempt it.
100→                    return
101→                }
102→
103→            visitFunctionSignature(node, emit, autoCorrect)
104→        }
105→    }
106→
107→    private fun ASTNode.functionSignatureNodes(): List<ASTNode> {
108→        // Find the signature including the element that has to be placed on the same line as the function signature
109→        //     fun foo(bar: String) {
110→        // or
111→        //     fun foo(bar: String) =
112→        val firstCodeChild = getFirstCodeChild()
113→        val startOfBodyBlock =
114→            this
115→                .findChildByType(BLOCK)
116→                ?.firstChildNode
117→        val startOfBodyExpression = this.findChildByType(EQ)
118→        return collectLeavesRecursively()
119→            .childrenBetween(
120→                startASTNodePredicate = { it == firstCodeChild },
121→                endASTNodePredicate = { it == startOfBodyBlock || it == startOfBodyExpression },
122→            )
123→    }
124→
125→    private fun ASTNode.getFirstCodeChild(): ASTNode? {
126→        val funNode = if (elementType == FUN_KEYWORD) {
127→            this.treeParent
128→        } else {
129→            this
130→        }
131→        funNode
132→            ?.findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
133→            ?.let { modifierList ->
134→                val iterator = modifierList.children().iterator()
135→                var currentNode: ASTNode
136→                while (iterator.hasNext()) {
137→                    currentNode = iterator.next()
138→                    if (currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
139→                        currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY &&
140→                        currentNode.elementType != WHITE_SPACE
141→                    ) {
142→                        return currentNode
143→                    }
144→                }
145→                return modifierList.nextCodeSibling()
146→            }
147→        return funNode.nextCodeLeaf()
148→    }
149→
150→    private fun visitFunctionSignature(
151→        node: ASTNode,
152→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
153→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
154→    ) {
155→        require(node.elementType == FUN)
156→
157→        val forceMultilineSignature =
158→            node.hasMinimumNumberOfParameters() ||
159→                node.containsParameterPrecededByAnnotationOnSeparateLine()
160→        if (isMaxLineLengthSet()) {
161→            val singleLineFunctionSignatureLength = calculateFunctionSignatureLengthAsSingleLineSignature(node, emit, autoCorrect)
162→            // Function signatures not having parameters, should not be reformatted automatically. It would result in function signatures
163→            // like below, which are not acceptable:
164→            //     fun aVeryLongFunctionName(
165→            //     ) = "some-value"
166→            //
167→            //     fun aVeryLongFunctionName(
168→            //     ): SomeVeryLongTypeName =
169→            //         SomeVeryLongTypeName(...)
170→            // Leave it up to the max-line-length rule to detect those violations so that the developer can handle it manually.
171→            val rewriteFunctionSignatureWithParameters = node.countParameters() > 0 && singleLineFunctionSignatureLength > maxLineLength
172→            if (forceMultilineSignature || rewriteFunctionSignatureWithParameters) {
173→                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = true, dryRun = false)
174→                // Due to rewriting the function signature, the remaining length on the last line of the multiline
175→                // signature needs to be recalculated
176→                val lengthOfLastLine = recalculateRemainLengthForFirstLineOfBodyExpression(node)
177→                fixFunctionBody(node, emit, autoCorrect, maxLineLength - lengthOfLastLine)
178→            } else {
179→                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = false)
180→                fixFunctionBody(node, emit, autoCorrect, maxLineLength - singleLineFunctionSignatureLength)
181→            }
182→        } else {
183→            // When max line length is not set then keep it as single line function signature only when the original
184→            // signature already was a single line signature. Otherwise, rewrite the entire signature as a multiline
185→            // signature.
186→            val rewriteToSingleLineFunctionSignature = node
187→                .functionSignatureNodes()
188→                .none { it.textContains('\n') }
189→            if (!forceMultilineSignature && rewriteToSingleLineFunctionSignature) {
190→                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = false)
191→            } else {
192→                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = true, dryRun = false)
193→            }
194→        }
195→    }
196→
197→    private fun recalculateRemainLengthForFirstLineOfBodyExpression(node: ASTNode): Int {
198→        val closingParenthesis =
199→            node
200→                .findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
201→                ?.findChildByType(RPAR)
202→        val tailNodesOfFunctionSignature = node
203→            .functionSignatureNodes()
204→            .childrenBetween(
205→                startASTNodePredicate = { it == closingParenthesis },
206→                endASTNodePredicate = { false },
207→            )
208→
209→        return node.lineIndent().length +
210→            tailNodesOfFunctionSignature.sumOf { it.text.length }
211→    }
212→
213→    private fun ASTNode.containsParameterPrecededByAnnotationOnSeparateLine(): Boolean =
214→        findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
215→            ?.children()
216→            .orEmpty()
217→            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
218→            .mapNotNull {
219→                // If the value parameter contains a modifier then this list is followed by a white space
220→                it.findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)?.nextSibling { true }
221→            }.any { it.textContains('\n') }
222→
223→    private fun calculateFunctionSignatureLengthAsSingleLineSignature(
224→        node: ASTNode,
225→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
226→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
227→    ): Int {
228→        val actualFunctionSignatureLength = node.getFunctionSignatureLength()
229→
230→        // Calculate the length of the function signature in case it would be rewritten as single line (and without a
231→        // maximum line length). The white space correction will be calculated via a dry run of the actual fix.
232→        return actualFunctionSignatureLength +
233→            // Calculate the white space correction in case the signature would be rewritten to a single line
234→            fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = true)
235→    }
236→
237→    private fun ASTNode.getFunctionSignatureLength() = lineIndent().length + getFunctionSignatureNodesLength()
238→
239→    private fun ASTNode.getFunctionSignatureNodesLength() =
240→        functionSignatureNodes()
241→            .joinTextToString()
242→            .length
243→
244→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(
245→        node: ASTNode,
246→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
247→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
248→        multiline: Boolean,
249→        dryRun: Boolean,
250→    ): Int {
251→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
252→
253→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
254→        val firstParameterInList =
255→            valueParameterList
256→                .children()
257→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
258→
259→        whiteSpaceCorrection += if (firstParameterInList == null) {
260→            // handle empty parameter list
261→            fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, dryRun)
262→        } else {
263→            fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
264→                fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
265→                fixWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun)
266→        }
267→
268→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
269→    }
270→
271→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(
272→        node: ASTNode,
273→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
274→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
275→        dryRun: Boolean,
276→    ): Int {
277→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
278→
279→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
280→
281→        valueParameterList
282→            .children()
283→            .filter { it.elementType != LPAR && it.elementType != RPAR }
284→            .also { elementsInValueParameterList ->
285→                // Functions with comments in the value parameter list are excluded from processing before. So an "empty" value
286→                // parameter list should only contain a single whitespace element
287→                require(elementsInValueParameterList.count() <= 1)
288→            }
289→            .firstOrNull()
290→            ?.let { whiteSpace ->
291→                if (!dryRun) {
292→                    emit(
293→                        whiteSpace.startOffset,
294→                        "No whitespace expected in empty parameter list",
295→                        true,
296→                    )
297→                }
298→                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
299→                    whiteSpace.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpace)
300→                } else {
301→                    whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpace.textLength
302→                }
303→            }
304→
305→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
306→    }
307→
308→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(
309→        node: ASTNode,
310→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
311→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
312→        multiline: Boolean,
313→        dryRun: Boolean,
314→    ): Int {
315→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
316→
317→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
318→        val firstParameterInList =
319→            valueParameterList
320→                .children()
321→                .first { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
322→
323→        val firstParameter = firstParameterInList.firstChildNode
324→        firstParameter
325→            ?.prevLeaf()
326→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
327→            .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
328→                if (multiline) {
329→                    val expectedParameterIndent = "\n" + node.lineIndent() + indent
330→                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
331→                        whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != expectedParameterIndent
332→                    ) {
333→                        if (!dryRun) {
334→                            emit(
335→                                firstParameterInList.startOffset,
336→                                "Newline expected after opening parenthesis",
337→                                true,
338→                            )
339→                        }
340→                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
341→                            valueParameterList.firstChildNode.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(expectedParameterIndent)
342→                        } else {
343→                            whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
344→                        }
345→                    }
346→                } else {
347→                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier != null) {
348→                        if (!dryRun) {
349→                            emit(
350→                                firstParameter!!.startOffset,
351→                                "No whitespace expected between opening parenthesis and first parameter name",
352→                                true,
353→                            )
354→                        }
355→                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
356→                            whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier)
357→                        } else {
358→                            whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.textLength
359→                        }
360→                    }
361→                }
362→            }
363→
364→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
365→    }

367→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(
368→        node: ASTNode,
369→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
370→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
371→        multiline: Boolean,
372→        dryRun: Boolean,
373→    ): Int {
374→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
375→
376→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
377→        val firstParameterInList =
378→            valueParameterList
379→                .children()
380→                .first { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
381→
382→        valueParameterList
383→            .children()
384→            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
385→            .filter { it != firstParameterInList }
386→            .forEach { valueParameter ->
387→                val firstChildNodeInValueParameter = valueParameter.firstChildNode
388→                firstChildNodeInValueParameter
389→                    ?.prevLeaf()
390→                    ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
391→                    .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
392→                        if (multiline) {
393→                            val expectedParameterIndent = "\n" + node.lineIndent() + indent
394→                            if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
395→                                whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != expectedParameterIndent
396→                            ) {
397→                                if (!dryRun) {
398→                                    emit(
399→                                        valueParameter.startOffset,
400→                                        "Parameter should start on a newline",
401→                                        true,
402→                                    )
403→                                }
404→                                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
405→                                    firstChildNodeInValueParameter.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedParameterIndent)
406→                                } else {
407→                                    whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
408→                                }
409→                            }
410→                        } else {
411→                            if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null || whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != " ") {
412→                                if (!dryRun) {
413→                                    emit(
414→                                        firstChildNodeInValueParameter!!.startOffset,
415→                                        "Single whitespace expected before parameter",
416→                                        true,
417→                                    )
418→                                }
419→                                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
420→                                    firstChildNodeInValueParameter.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
421→                                } else {
422→                                    whiteSpaceCorrection += 1 - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
423→                                }
424→                            }
425→                        }
426→                    }
427→            }
428→
429→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
430→    }

432→    private fun fixWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis(
433→        node: ASTNode,
434→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
435→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
436→        multiline: Boolean,
437→        dryRun: Boolean,
438→    ): Int {
439→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
440→
441→        val newlineAndIndent = "\n" + node.lineIndent()
442→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(node.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
443→
444→        val closingParenthesis = valueParameterList.findChildByType(RPAR)
445→        closingParenthesis
446→            ?.prevSibling { true }
447→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
448→            .let { whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis ->
449→                if (multiline) {
450→                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis == null ||
451→                        whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.text != newlineAndIndent
452→                    ) {
453→                        if (!dryRun) {
454→                            emit(
455→                                closingParenthesis!!.startOffset,
456→                                "Newline expected before closing parenthesis",
457→                                true,
458→                            )
459→                        }
460→                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
461→                            closingParenthesis!!.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(newlineAndIndent)
462→                        } else {
463→                            whiteSpaceCorrection += newlineAndIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis?.textLength ?: 0)
464→                        }
465→                    }
466→                } else {
467→                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis != null &&
468→                        whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.nextLeaf()?.elementType == RPAR
469→                    ) {
470→                        if (!dryRun) {
471→                            emit(
472→                                whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.startOffset,
473→                                "No whitespace expected between last parameter and closing parenthesis",
474→                                true,
475→                            )
476→                        }
477→                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
478→                            whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis)
479→                        } else {
480→                            whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.textLength
481→                        }
482→                    }
483→                }
484→            }
485→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
486→    }

488→    private fun fixFunctionBody(
489→        node: ASTNode,
490→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
491→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
492→        maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression: Int,
493→    ) {
494→        if (node.findChildByType(EQ) == null) {
495→            fixFunctionBodyBlock(node, emit, autoCorrect)
496→        } else {
497→            fixFunctionBodyExpression(node, emit, autoCorrect, maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression)

499→    }
500→
501→    private fun fixFunctionBodyExpression(
502→        node: ASTNode,
503→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
504→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
505→        maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression: Int,
506→    ) {
507→        val lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression =
508→            node
509→                .findChildByType(EQ)
510→                ?.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
511→                ?: return
512→        val bodyNodes = node.getFunctionBody(lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression)
513→        val whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression = bodyNodes.getStartingWhitespaceOrNull()
514→        val functionBodyExpressionNodes = bodyNodes.dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
515→
516→        val functionBodyExpressionLines = functionBodyExpressionNodes
517→            .joinTextToString()
518→            .split("\n")
519→        functionBodyExpressionLines
520→            .firstOrNull()
521→            ?.also { firstLineOfBodyExpression ->
522→                if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()) {
523→                    val mergeWithFunctionSignature =
524→                        if (firstLineOfBodyExpression.length < maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression) {
525→                            functionBodyExpressionWrapping == default ||
526→                                (functionBodyExpressionWrapping == multiline && functionBodyExpressionLines.size == 1) ||
527→                                node.isMultilineFunctionSignatureWithoutExplicitReturnType(
528→                                    lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression,
529→                                )
530→                        } else {
531→                            false
532→                        }
533→                    if (mergeWithFunctionSignature) {
534→                        emit(
535→                            whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression!!.startOffset,
536→                            "First line of body expression fits on same line as function signature",
537→                            true,
538→                        )
539→                        if (autoCorrect) {
540→                            (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression as LeafPsiElement).rawReplaceWithText(" ")

542→                    }
543→                } else if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression == null ||
544→                    !whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression.textContains('\n')
545→                ) {
546→                    if (node.isMultilineFunctionSignatureWithoutExplicitReturnType(lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression) &&
547→                        firstLineOfBodyExpression.length + 1 <= maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression
548→                    ) {
549→                        if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression == null ||
550→                            whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression.text != " "
551→                        ) {
552→                            emit(
553→                                functionBodyExpressionNodes.first().startOffset,
554→                                "Single whitespace expected before expression body",
555→                                true,
556→                            )
557→                            if (autoCorrect) {
558→                                functionBodyExpressionNodes
559→                                    .first()
560→                                    .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
561→                            }
562→                        }
563→                    } else if (firstLineOfBodyExpression.length + 1 > maxLengthRemainingForFirstLineOfBodyExpression ||
564→                        (functionBodyExpressionWrapping == multiline && functionBodyExpressionLines.size > 1) ||
565→                        functionBodyExpressionWrapping == always

567→                        emit(
568→                            functionBodyExpressionNodes.first().startOffset,
569→                            "Newline expected before expression body",
570→                            true,
571→                        )
572→                        if (autoCorrect) {
573→                            functionBodyExpressionNodes
574→                                .first()
575→                                .upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe("\n" + node.lineIndent() + indent)
576→                        }
577→                    }
578→                }
579→            }
580→    }

582→    private fun ASTNode.isMultilineFunctionSignatureWithoutExplicitReturnType(lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression: ASTNode?) =
583→        functionSignatureNodes()
584→            .childrenBetween(
585→                startASTNodePredicate = { true },
586→                endASTNodePredicate = { it == lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBodyExpression },
587→            ).joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }
588→            .split("\n")
589→            .lastOrNull()
590→            ?.matches(INDENT_WITH_CLOSING_PARENTHESIS)
591→            ?: false
592→
593→    private fun fixFunctionBodyBlock(
594→        node: ASTNode,
595→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
596→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
597→    ) {
598→        val lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBlockBody =
599→            node

601→                ?.nextLeaf(includeEmpty = true)
602→                ?: return
603→
604→        val bodyNodes = node.getFunctionBody(lastNodeOfFunctionSignatureWithBlockBody)
605→        val whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression = bodyNodes.getStartingWhitespaceOrNull()
606→        val functionBodyBlock = bodyNodes.dropWhile { it.isWhiteSpace() }
607→
608→        functionBodyBlock
609→            .joinTextToString()
610→            .split("\n")
611→            .firstOrNull()
612→            ?.also {
613→                if (whiteSpaceBeforeFunctionBodyExpression?.text != " ") {
614→                    emit(functionBodyBlock.first().startOffset, "Expected a single space before body block", true)
615→                    if (autoCorrect) {
616→                        functionBodyBlock
617→                            .first()
618→                            .prevLeaf(true)

620→                    }
621→                }
622→            }
623→    }

625→    private fun ASTNode.getFunctionBody(splitNode: ASTNode?): List<ASTNode> =
626→        this

629→                startASTNodePredicate = { it == splitNode },
630→                endASTNodePredicate = {
631→                    // collect all remaining nodes
632→                    false
633→                },

643→    private fun List<ASTNode>.collectLeavesRecursively(): List<ASTNode> = flatMap { it.collectLeavesRecursively() }
644→
645→    private fun ASTNode.collectLeavesRecursively(): List<ASTNode> =
646→        if (psi is LeafElement) {
647→            listOf(this)
648→        } else {
649→            children()
650→                .flatMap { it.collectLeavesRecursively() }
651→                .toList()

654→    private fun List<ASTNode>.childrenBetween(
655→        startASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean = { _ -> true },
656→        endASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean = { _ -> false },
657→    ): List<ASTNode> {
658→        val iterator = iterator()
659→        var currentNode: ASTNode
660→        val childrenBetween: MutableList<ASTNode> = mutableListOf()
661→
662→        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
663→            currentNode = iterator.next()
664→            if (startASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
665→                childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
666→                break
667→            }
668→        }
669→
670→        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
671→            currentNode = iterator.next()
672→            childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
673→            if (endASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
674→                break
675→            }
676→        }
677→
678→        return childrenBetween
679→    }
680→
681→    private fun List<ASTNode>.joinTextToString(block: (ASTNode) -> String = { it.text }): String =
682→        collectLeavesRecursively().joinToString(separator = "") { block(it) }

684→    private fun ASTNode.hasMinimumNumberOfParameters(): Boolean = countParameters() >= functionSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters

686→    private fun ASTNode.countParameters(): Int {

701→            EditorConfigProperty(
702→                type = PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
703→                    "ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than",
704→                    "Force wrapping the parameters of the function signature in case it contains at least the specified " +
705→                        "number of parameters even in case the entire function signature would fit on a single line. " +
706→                        "By default this parameter is not enabled.",
707→                    PropertyType.PropertyValueParser.POSITIVE_INT_VALUE_PARSER,
708→                    setOf("1", "2", "3", "4", "5", "6", "7", "8", "9", "unset"),
709→                ),
710→                defaultValue = Int.MAX_VALUE,
711→                ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = 2,
712→            )
713→
714→        public val FUNCTION_BODY_EXPRESSION_WRAPPING_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<FunctionBodyExpressionWrapping> =
715→            EditorConfigProperty(
716→                type = PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
717→                    "ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping",
718→                    "Determines how to wrap the body of function in case it is an expression. Use 'default' " +
719→                        "to wrap the body expression only when the first line of the expression does not fit on the same " +
720→                        "line as the function signature. Use 'multiline' to force wrapping of body expressions that " +
721→                        "consists of multiple line. Use 'always' to force wrapping of body expression always.",
722→                    EnumValueParser(FunctionBodyExpressionWrapping::class.java),
723→                    FunctionBodyExpressionWrapping.values().map { it.name }.toSet(),
724→                ),
725→                defaultValue = default,
726→                ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = multiline,
727→            )
728→
729→        private val INDENT_WITH_CLOSING_PARENTHESIS = Regex("\\s*\\) =")
730→    }
731→
732→    /**
733→     * Code style to be used while linting and formatting. Note that the [EnumValueParser] requires values to be lowercase.
734→     */
735→    @Suppress("EnumEntryName")
736→    public enum class FunctionBodyExpressionWrapping {
737→        /**

739→         * not exceeded.

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig
 2→
 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
 4→import mu.KotlinLogging
 5→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 6→
 7→private const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE = 100 // https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#line_wrapping
 8→private const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_KTLINT_OFFICIAL_CODE_STYLE = 140
 9→private const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF_EDITOR_CONFIG = "off"
10→
11→/**
12→ * Integer value that denotes that the property is to be considered as disabled.
13→ */
14→public const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF: Int = Int.MAX_VALUE
15→
16→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
17→private var isInvalidValueLoggedBefore = false
18→
19→public val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Int> =
20→    EditorConfigProperty(
21→        name = PropertyType.max_line_length.name,
22→        type = PropertyType.max_line_length,
23→        defaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF,
24→        androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE,
25→        intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF,
26→        ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_KTLINT_OFFICIAL_CODE_STYLE,
27→        propertyMapper = { property, codeStyleValue ->
28→            when {
29→                property == null || property.isUnset -> {
30→                    codeStyleValue.defaultValue()
31→                }
32→
33→                /**
34→                 * Internally, Ktlint uses integer 'Int.MAX_VALUE' to indicate that the max line length has to be ignored as this is easier
35→                 * in comparisons to check whether the maximum length of a line is exceeded.
36→                 */
37→                property.sourceValue == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF_EDITOR_CONFIG -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
38→
39→                else ->
40→                    PropertyType
41→                        .max_line_length
42→                        .parse(property.sourceValue)
43→                        .let {
44→                            if (!it.isValid) {
45→                                if (!isInvalidValueLoggedBefore) {
46→                                    isInvalidValueLoggedBefore = true
47→                                    LOGGER.warn { "Found invalid '.editorconfig' property value: ${it.errorMessage}" }
48→                                }
49→                                if (it.source == "-1") {
50→                                    MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
51→                                } else {
52→                                    codeStyleValue.defaultValue()
53→                                }
54→                            } else {
55→                                it.parsed
56→                            }
57→                        }
58→            }
59→        },
60→        propertyWriter = { property ->
61→            if (property <= 0 || property == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF) {
62→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF_EDITOR_CONFIG
63→            } else {
64→                property.toString()
65→            }
66→        },
67→    )
68→
69→private fun CodeStyleValue.defaultValue() =
70→    when (this) {
71→        CodeStyleValue.android -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE
72→        CodeStyleValue.android_studio -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE
73→        CodeStyleValue.official -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
74→        CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
75→        CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_KTLINT_OFFICIAL_CODE_STYLE
76→    }

content.thinking:
Now I understand the issue better. The `FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule` always tries to put the return type on the same line as the function signature, with only a single space between the colon and the return type. This can cause issues when the function name is already very long, and putting the return type on the same line would exceed the max line length.

Looking at how `FunctionSignatureRule` handles this, it:
1. Checks if max line length is set
2. Calculates the length of the function signature as a single line
3. Only reformats when it won't exceed max line length

I need to modify `FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule` similarly to:
1. Accept and use the `MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY`
2. Calculate the potential line length if the return type is moved to the same line
3. Only report/fix the spacing if it won't exceed the max line length

Let me also look at the test file for this rule to understand the expected behavior better.

content.thinking:
Now I understand the issue better. The `FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule` tries to fix the whitespace after the colon by replacing it with a single space. But if the return type is on a new line to avoid exceeding max line length, this fix would cause the line to exceed max line length.

Looking at the `fixWhiteSpaceBetweenColonAndReturnType` method in `FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule`, it always reports "Single space expected between colon and return type" if the whitespace after the colon is not a single space, and tries to fix it by upserting a single space.

The fix should be to:
1. Make the rule aware of the max line length property
2. When the whitespace after the colon contains a newline, check if putting the return type on the same line would exceed max line length

 25→
 26→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 27→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 28→    }
 29→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 30→        node: ASTNode,
 31→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 32→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 33→    ) {
 34→        node.firstChildNode
 35→        node
 36→            .takeIf { node.elementType == FUN }
 37→            ?.let { node.findChildByType(COLON) }
 38→            ?.let { colonNode ->
 39→                removeWhiteSpaceBetweenClosingParenthesisAndColon(colonNode, emit, autoCorrect)
 40→                fixWhiteSpaceBetweenColonAndReturnType(colonNode, emit, autoCorrect)
 41→            }
 42→    }
 43→
 44→    private fun removeWhiteSpaceBetweenClosingParenthesisAndColon(
 45→        node: ASTNode,
 46→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 47→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 48→    ) {
 49→        require(node.elementType == COLON)
 50→        node
 51→            .prevLeaf()
 52→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
 53→            ?.let { whitespaceBeforeColonNode ->
 54→                emit(whitespaceBeforeColonNode.startOffset, "Unexpected whitespace", true)
 55→                if (autoCorrect) {
 56→                    whitespaceBeforeColonNode.treeParent?.removeChild(whitespaceBeforeColonNode)
 57→                }
 58→            }
 59→    }
 60→
 61→    private fun fixWhiteSpaceBetweenColonAndReturnType(
 62→        node: ASTNode,
 63→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 64→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 65→    ) {
 66→        require(node.elementType == COLON)
 67→        node
 68→            .nextLeaf()
 69→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
 70→            .let { whiteSpaceAfterColon ->
 71→                if (whiteSpaceAfterColon?.text != " ") {
 72→                    // In case the whitespace contains a newline, check if putting everything on one line
 73→                    // would exceed the max line length. If so, leave it as is to avoid violating max-line-length rule.
 74→                    if (whiteSpaceAfterColon != null &&
 75→                        whiteSpaceAfterColon.textContains('\n') &&
 76→                        maxLineLength != MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
 77→                    ) {
 78→                        val lineLengthIfSingleLine = calculateLineLengthIfSingleLine(node, whiteSpaceAfterColon)
 79→                        if (lineLengthIfSingleLine > maxLineLength) {
 80→                            return
 81→                        }
 82→                    }
 83→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Single space expected between colon and return type", true)
 84→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 85→                        node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
 86→                    }
 87→                }
 88→            }
 89→    }
 90→
 91→    private fun calculateLineLengthIfSingleLine(colonNode: ASTNode, whiteSpaceAfterColon: ASTNode): Int {
 92→        // Calculate the line length if we were to put the return type on the same line as the function signature.
 93→        // Line length = position after colon + 1 (for space) + length of text from return type to next newline
 94→        val lengthBeforeColon = colonNode.column - 1
 95→        val colonLength = colonNode.textLength
 96→        val spaceLength = 1
 97→
 98→        // Calculate length of text after whitespace until the next newline
 99→        var textAfterLength = 0
100→        var leaf = whiteSpaceAfterColon.nextLeaf()
101→        while (leaf != null) {
102→            if (leaf.elementType == WHITE_SPACE && leaf.textContains('\n')) {
103→                break
104→            }
105→            textAfterLength += leaf.textLength
106→            leaf = leaf.nextLeaf()
107→        }
108→
109→        return lengthBeforeColon + colonLength + spaceLength + textAfterLength
110→    }
111→}

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.COLON
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.FUN
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHITE_SPACE
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.column
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 16→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 17→
 18→public class FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule :
 19→    StandardRule(
 20→        id = "function-return-type-spacing",
 21→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY),
 22→    ),
 23→    Rule.Experimental {
 24→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 25→
 26→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 27→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 28→    }
 29→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 30→        node: ASTNode,
 31→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 32→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 33→    ) {
 34→        node.firstChildNode
 35→        node
 36→            .takeIf { node.elementType == FUN }
 37→            ?.let { node.findChildByType(COLON) }
 38→            ?.let { colonNode ->
 39→                removeWhiteSpaceBetweenClosingParenthesisAndColon(colonNode, emit, autoCorrect)
 40→                fixWhiteSpaceBetweenColonAndReturnType(colonNode, emit, autoCorrect)
 41→            }
 42→    }
 43→
 44→    private fun removeWhiteSpaceBetweenClosingParenthesisAndColon(
 45→        node: ASTNode,
 46→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 47→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 48→    ) {
 49→        require(node.elementType == COLON)
 50→        node
 51→            .prevLeaf()
 52→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
 53→            ?.let { whitespaceBeforeColonNode ->
 54→                emit(whitespaceBeforeColonNode.startOffset, "Unexpected whitespace", true)
 55→                if (autoCorrect) {
 56→                    whitespaceBeforeColonNode.treeParent?.removeChild(whitespaceBeforeColonNode)
 57→                }
 58→            }
 59→    }
 60→
 61→    private fun fixWhiteSpaceBetweenColonAndReturnType(
 62→        node: ASTNode,
 63→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 64→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 65→    ) {
 66→        require(node.elementType == COLON)
 67→        node
 68→            .nextLeaf()
 69→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
 70→            .let { whiteSpaceAfterColon ->
 71→                if (whiteSpaceAfterColon?.text != " ") {
 72→                    // In case the whitespace contains a newline, check if putting everything on one line
 73→                    // would exceed the max line length. If so, leave it as is to avoid violating max-line-length rule.
 74→                    if (whiteSpaceAfterColon != null &&
 75→                        whiteSpaceAfterColon.textContains('\n') &&
 76→                        maxLineLength != MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
 77→                    ) {
 78→                        val lineLengthIfSingleLine = calculateLineLengthIfSingleLine(node, whiteSpaceAfterColon)
 79→                        if (lineLengthIfSingleLine > maxLineLength) {
 80→                            return
 81→                        }
 82→                    }
 83→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Single space expected between colon and return type", true)
 84→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 85→                        node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
 86→                    }
 87→                }
 88→            }
 89→    }
 90→
 91→    private fun calculateLineLengthIfSingleLine(colonNode: ASTNode, whiteSpaceAfterColon: ASTNode): Int {
 92→        // Calculate the line length if we were to put the return type on the same line as the function signature.
 93→        // Line length = position after colon + 1 (for space) + length of text from return type to next newline
 94→        val lengthBeforeColon = colonNode.column - 1
 95→        val colonLength = colonNode.textLength
 96→        val spaceLength = 1
 97→
 98→        // Calculate length of text after whitespace until the next newline
 99→        var textAfterLength = 0
100→        var leaf = whiteSpaceAfterColon.nextLeaf()
101→        while (leaf != null) {
102→            if (leaf.elementType == WHITE_SPACE && leaf.textContains('\n')) {
103→                break
104→            }
105→            textAfterLength += leaf.textLength
106→            leaf = leaf.nextLeaf()
107→        }
108→
109→        return lengthBeforeColon + colonLength + spaceLength + textAfterLength
110→    }
111→}
112→
113→public val FUNCTION_RETURN_TYPE_SPACING_RULE_ID: RuleId = FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule().ruleId
114→

conversation_history.content:
1→# Changelog
  2→All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.
  3→This project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
  4→
  5→## Unreleased
  6→
  7→WARNING: This version on KtLint contains a number of breaking changes in KtLint CLI and KtLint API. If you are using KtLint with custom ruleset jars or custom reporter jars, then those need to be upgrade before you can use them with this version of ktlint. Please contact the maintainers of those jars and ask them to upgrade a.s.a.p.
  8→
  9→All rule id's in the output of Ktlint are now prefixed with a rule set. Although KtLint currently supports standard rules to be unqualified, users are encouraged to migrate to include the rule set id in all references to rules. This includes following:
 10→- The `--disabled-rules` parameter in KtLint CLI and `.editorconfig`.
 11→- The `source` element in the KtLint CLI `baseline.xml` file. Regenerating this file, fixes all rule references automatically.
 12→- The KtLint disable directives `ktlint-enable` / `ktlint-disable` and the `@Suppress('ktlint:...')` annotations.
 13→
 14→### Moving experimental rules to standard rule set
 15→
 16→The `experimental` rule set has been merged with `standard` rule set. The rules which formerly were part of the `experimental` rule set are still being treated as before. The rules will only be run in case `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or in case the rule is explicitly enabled.
 17→
 18→Note that the prefix `experimental:` has to be removed from all references to this rule. Check references in:
 19→* The `.editorconfig` setting `disabled_rules`.
 20→* KtLint disable and enable directives.
 21→* The `VisitorModifier.RunAfterRule`.
 22→
 23→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
 24→
 25→#### Experimental rules
 26→
 27→Rules in custom rule sets can be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint whenever the `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled.
 28→
 29→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set. A rule is identified by its qualified rule id that includes the rule set id. Moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set has the downside that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
 30→
 31→#### EditorConfig
 32→
 33→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. Note that also new fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
 34→
 35→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
 36→
 37→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
 38→
 39→A new code style with name `ktlint_offical` has been added. This code style makes it possible to innovate ktlint beyond current expectations of users that are familiar with the existing code styles. 
 40→
 41→The `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This codestyle also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in the before mentioned styleguide. Also, this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor. In the long run, this code style becomes the default code style provided by KtLint.
 42→
 43→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on Kotlin Coding conventions (https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint. Note that in the long run, the default code style is changed to `ktlint_official`.
 44→
 45→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on Android's Kotlin styleguide (https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
 46→
 47→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
 48→
 49→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
 50→
 51→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
 52→
 53→Classes below have been moved to the new module `ktlint-rule-engine`:
 54→
 55→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine-core`                               |
 56→|----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 57→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig               | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig                            |
 58→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties     | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig                            |
 59→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.OptInFeatures              | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.OptInFeatures                           |
 60→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType                | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType                             |
 61→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.package                    | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ASTNodeExtension                        |
 62→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.IndentConfig                   | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig                            |
 63→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule                           | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule                                    |
 64→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleProvider                   | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleProvider                            |
 65→
 66→
 67→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine`                               |
 68→|----------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 69→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigDefaults       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.EditorConfigDefaults                    |
 70→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigOverride       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.EditorConfigOverride                    |
 71→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintParseException       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintParseException                    |
 72→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintRuleException        | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleException                     |
 73→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLint                         | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintRuleEngine                        |
 74→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.LintError                      | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.LintError                               |
 75→
 76→Class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLint.Code.CodeFile` has been replaced with factory method `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.Code.fromFile`. Likewise, class `com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLint.Code.CodeSnippet` has been replaced with factory method `com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.Code.fromSnippet`.
 77→
 78→Classes below have been moved to the new module `ktlint-cli-ruleset-core`:
 79→
 80→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli-ruleset-core`                           |
 81→|----------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
 82→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleSetProviderV2              | com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.ruleset.core.api.RuleSetProviderV3                   |
 83→
 84→
 85→Class below has been moved to the new module `ktlint-cli-reporter-core`:
 86→
 87→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`   | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli-reporter-core`     |
 88→|-------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------|
 89→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtlintVersion   | com.pinterest.ktlint.cli.reporter.core.api.KtlintVersion |
 90→
 91→Class below has been moved to the new module `ktlint-logger`:
 92→
 93→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`               | New class/package name in `ktlint-logger`                   |
 94→|-------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------|
 95→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.KtLintKLoggerInitializer.kt | com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.KtLintKLoggerInitializer.kt |
 96→
 97→Module `ktlint` has been renamed to `ktlint-cli`. Classes below have been relocated in this module:
 98→
 99→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core` | New class/package name in `ktlint-cli` |
100→|-----------------------------------------|----------------------------------------|

220→
221→#### `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` default value
222→
223→Previously the default value for `.editorconfig` property `max_line_length` was set to `-1` in ktlint unless the property was defined explicitly in the `.editorconfig` or when `ktlint_code_style` was set to Android. As a result of that rules have to check that max_line_length contains a positive value before checking that the actual line length is exceeding the maximum. Now the value `Int.MAX_VALUE` (use constant `MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF` to refer to that value) is used instead. 
224→
225→### Added
226→
227→* Add new experimental rule for `ktlint_official` code style that disallows a class to start with a blank line `no-empty-first-line-in-class-body`.
228→* Add new experimental rule for `ktlint_official` code style that enforces consistent usage of braces in all branches of a singe if or if-else-if statement `if-else-bracing`.
229→* Add new experimental rule for `ktlint_official` code style that disallows consecutive comments except EOL comments `no-consecutive-comments`
230→
231→### Removed
232→
233→* Remove support of the `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules`. See [disabled rules](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/rules/configuration-ktlint/#disabled-rules) for more information.
234→
235→### Fixed
236→
237→* An enumeration class having a primary constructor and in which the list of enum entries is followed by a semicolon then do not remove the semicolon in case it is followed by code element `no-semi` ([#1733](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1733))
238→* Do not add the (first line of the) body expression on the same line as the function signature in case the max line length would be exceeded. `function-signature`. 
239→* Do not add the first line of a multiline body expression on the same line as the function signature in case function body expression wrapping property is set to `multiline`. `function-signature`. 
240→* Disable the `standard:filename` rule whenever Ktlint CLI is run with option `--stdin` ([#1742](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1742))
241→* The parameters of a function literal containing a multiline parameter list are aligned with first parameter whenever the first parameter is on the same line as the start of that function literal (not allowed in `ktlint_official` code style) `indent` ([#1756](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1756)).
242→* Do not throw exception when enum class does not contain entries `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` ([#1711](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1711))
243→* Fix continuation indent for a dot qualified array access expression in `ktlint_official` code style only `indent` ([#1540](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1540)).
244→* When generating the `.editorconfig` use value `off` for the `max_line_length` property instead of value `-1` to denote that lines are not restricted to a maximum length ([#1824](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1824)).
245→* Do not report an "unnecessary semicolon" after adding a trailing comma to an enum class containing a code element after the last enum entry `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` ([#1786](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1786))
246→
247→### Changed
248→* Wrap the parameters of a function literal containing a multiline parameter list (only in `ktlint_official` code style) `parameter-list-wrapping` ([#1681](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1681)).
249→  Changelog:
250→* KtLint CLI exits with an error in any of following cases (this list is not exhaustive):
251→  - A custom ruleset jar is to be loaded and that jar contains a deprecated RuleSetProviderV2.
252→  - A custom ruleset jar is to be loaded and that jar does not contain the required RuleSetProviderV3.
253→  - A custom reporter jar is to be loaded and that jar contains a deprecated ReporterProvider.
254→  - A custom reporter jar is to be loaded and that jar does not contain the required ReporterProviderV2.
255→* Disable the default patterns if the option `--patterns-from-stdin` is specified ([#1793](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/1793))
256→* Update Kotlin development version to `1.8.20-Beta` and Kotlin version to `1.8.10`.
257→* Revert to matrix build to speed up build, especially for the Windows related build ([#1787](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/pull/1787))
258→
259→## [0.48.2] - 2023-01-21
260→
261→### Additional clarification on API Changes in `0.48.0` and `0.48.1`
262→
263→Starting with Ktlint `0.48.x`, rule and rule sets can be enabled/disabled with a separate property per rule (set). Please read [deprecation of (ktlint_)disable_rules property](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/faq/#why-is-editorconfig-property-disabled_rules-deprecated-and-how-do-i-resolve-this) for more information.
264→
265→API Consumers that provide experimental rules to the KtLintRuleEngine, must also enable the experimental rules or instruct their users to do so in the `.editorconfig` file. From the perspective of the API Consumer it might be confusing or unnecessary to do so as the experimental rule was already provided explicitly.
266→
267→Ktlint wants to provide the user (e.g. a developer) a uniform and consistent user experience. The `.editorconfig` becomes more and more central to store configuration for Ktlint. This to ensure that all team members use the exact same configuration when running ktlint regardless whether the Ktlint CLI or an API Consumer is being used.
268→
269→The `.editorconfig` is a powerful configuration tool which can be used in very different ways. Most projects use a single `.editorconfig` file containing one common section for kotlin and kotlin scripts files. For example, the `.editorconfig` file of the Ktlint project contains following section:

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.rules
  2→
  3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.COLON
  4→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.FUN
  5→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType.WHITE_SPACE
  6→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule
  7→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleId
  8→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.column
  9→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.EditorConfig
 10→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY
 11→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig.MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
 12→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.nextLeaf
 13→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.prevLeaf
 14→import com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe
 15→import com.pinterest.ktlint.ruleset.standard.StandardRule
 16→import org.jetbrains.kotlin.com.intellij.lang.ASTNode
 17→
 18→public class FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule :
 19→    StandardRule(
 20→        id = "function-return-type-spacing",
 21→        usesEditorConfigProperties = setOf(MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY),
 22→    ),
 23→    Rule.Experimental {
 24→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 25→
 26→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 27→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 28→    }
 29→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 30→        node: ASTNode,
 31→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 32→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 33→    ) {
 34→        node.firstChildNode
 35→        node
 36→            .takeIf { node.elementType == FUN }
 37→            ?.let { node.findChildByType(COLON) }
 38→            ?.let { colonNode ->
 39→                removeWhiteSpaceBetweenClosingParenthesisAndColon(colonNode, emit, autoCorrect)
 40→                fixWhiteSpaceBetweenColonAndReturnType(colonNode, emit, autoCorrect)
 41→            }
 42→    }
 43→
 44→    private fun removeWhiteSpaceBetweenClosingParenthesisAndColon(
 45→        node: ASTNode,
 46→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 47→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 48→    ) {
 49→        require(node.elementType == COLON)
 50→        node
 51→            .prevLeaf()
 52→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
 53→            ?.let { whitespaceBeforeColonNode ->
 54→                emit(whitespaceBeforeColonNode.startOffset, "Unexpected whitespace", true)
 55→                if (autoCorrect) {
 56→                    whitespaceBeforeColonNode.treeParent?.removeChild(whitespaceBeforeColonNode)
 57→                }
 58→            }
 59→    }
 60→
 61→    private fun fixWhiteSpaceBetweenColonAndReturnType(
 62→        node: ASTNode,
 63→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 64→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 65→    ) {
 66→        require(node.elementType == COLON)
 67→        node
 68→            .nextLeaf()
 69→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
 70→            .let { whiteSpaceAfterColon ->
 71→                if (whiteSpaceAfterColon?.text != " ") {
 72→                    // In case the whitespace contains a newline, check if putting everything on one line
 73→                    // would exceed the max line length. If so, leave it as is to avoid violating max-line-length rule.
 74→                    if (whiteSpaceAfterColon != null &&
 75→                        whiteSpaceAfterColon.textContains('\n') &&
 76→                        maxLineLength != MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
 77→                    ) {
 78→                        val lineLengthIfSingleLine = calculateLineLengthIfSingleLine(node, whiteSpaceAfterColon)
 79→                        if (lineLengthIfSingleLine > maxLineLength) {
 80→                            return
 81→                        }
 82→                    }
 83→                    emit(node.startOffset, "Single space expected between colon and return type", true)
 84→                    if (autoCorrect) {
 85→                        node.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
 86→                    }
 87→                }
 88→            }
 89→    }
 90→
 91→    private fun calculateLineLengthIfSingleLine(colonNode: ASTNode, whiteSpaceAfterColon: ASTNode): Int {
 92→        // Calculate the line length if we were to put the return type on the same line as the function signature.
 93→        // Line length = position after colon + 1 (for space) + length of text from return type to next newline
 94→        val lengthBeforeColon = colonNode.column - 1
 95→        val colonLength = colonNode.textLength
 96→        val spaceLength = 1
 97→
 98→        // Calculate length of text after whitespace until the next newline
 99→        var textAfterLength = 0
100→        var leaf = whiteSpaceAfterColon.nextLeaf()
101→        while (leaf != null) {
102→            if (leaf.elementType == WHITE_SPACE && leaf.textContains('\n')) {
103→                break
104→            }
105→            textAfterLength += leaf.textLength
106→            leaf = leaf.nextLeaf()
107→        }
108→
109→        return lengthBeforeColon + colonLength + spaceLength + textAfterLength
110→    }
111→}
112→
113→public val FUNCTION_RETURN_TYPE_SPACING_RULE_ID: RuleId = FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule().ruleId
114→

content.text:
All changes have been made. Let me provide a summary of what was implemented:

## Summary

The issue was that the `function-return-type-spacing` rule was reformatting code in a way that could violate the `max-line-length` rule. When a function had its return type on a new line to avoid exceeding max line length, the rule would move it back to the same line, causing a max line length violation.

### Changes Made

1. **`FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRule.kt`**:
   - Added `MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY` as an editor config property that the rule uses
   - Added `beforeFirstNode` method to load the max line length from editor config
   - Modified `fixWhiteSpaceBetweenColonAndReturnType` to check if the whitespace after the colon contains a newline. If so, it calculates the potential line length and only reports/fixes the spacing if it wouldn't exceed max line length
   - Added `calculateLineLengthIfSingleLine` helper method to calculate what the line length would be if the return type were moved to the same line

2. **`FunctionReturnTypeSpacingRuleTest.kt`**:
   - Added test cases to verify the new behavior:
     - When return type on a new line would exceed max line length when put on same line → no reformat
     - When return type on a new line would NOT exceed max line length when put on same line → reformat as usual
     - Whitespace before the colon is still removed regardless of max line length (the fix only applies to whitespace after the colon that contains a newline)

3. **`CHANGELOG.md`**:
   - Added changelog entry documenting this fix

### How It Works

The fix works by:
1. Checking if the whitespace after the colon contains a newline (indicating the return type is on a new line)
2. If it does, and max line length is set, calculating what the line length would be if we put everything on one line
3. Only reformatting if the resulting line length would not exceed max line length
4. If it would exceed, the rule leaves the code as-is, allowing the user to keep the return type on a separate line

1→package com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig
 2→
 3→import com.pinterest.ktlint.logger.api.initKtLintKLogger
 4→import mu.KotlinLogging
 5→import org.ec4j.core.model.PropertyType
 6→
 7→private const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE = 100 // https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide#line_wrapping
 8→private const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_KTLINT_OFFICIAL_CODE_STYLE = 140
 9→private const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF_EDITOR_CONFIG = "off"
10→
11→/**
12→ * Integer value that denotes that the property is to be considered as disabled.
13→ */
14→public const val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF: Int = Int.MAX_VALUE
15→
16→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
17→private var isInvalidValueLoggedBefore = false
18→
19→public val MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Int> =
20→    EditorConfigProperty(
21→        name = PropertyType.max_line_length.name,
22→        type = PropertyType.max_line_length,
23→        defaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF,
24→        androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE,
25→        intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF,
26→        ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_KTLINT_OFFICIAL_CODE_STYLE,
27→        propertyMapper = { property, codeStyleValue ->
28→            when {
29→                property == null || property.isUnset -> {
30→                    codeStyleValue.defaultValue()
31→                }
32→
33→                /**
34→                 * Internally, Ktlint uses integer 'Int.MAX_VALUE' to indicate that the max line length has to be ignored as this is easier
35→                 * in comparisons to check whether the maximum length of a line is exceeded.
36→                 */
37→                property.sourceValue == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF_EDITOR_CONFIG -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
38→
39→                else ->
40→                    PropertyType
41→                        .max_line_length
42→                        .parse(property.sourceValue)
43→                        .let {
44→                            if (!it.isValid) {
45→                                if (!isInvalidValueLoggedBefore) {
46→                                    isInvalidValueLoggedBefore = true
47→                                    LOGGER.warn { "Found invalid '.editorconfig' property value: ${it.errorMessage}" }
48→                                }
49→                                if (it.source == "-1") {
50→                                    MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
51→                                } else {
52→                                    codeStyleValue.defaultValue()
53→                                }
54→                            } else {
55→                                it.parsed
56→                            }
57→                        }
58→            }
59→        },
60→        propertyWriter = { property ->
61→            if (property <= 0 || property == MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF) {
62→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF_EDITOR_CONFIG
63→            } else {
64→                property.toString()
65→            }
66→        },
67→    )
68→
69→private fun CodeStyleValue.defaultValue() =
70→    when (this) {
71→        CodeStyleValue.android -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE
72→        CodeStyleValue.android_studio -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_ANDROID_STUDIO_CODE_STYLE
73→        CodeStyleValue.official -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
74→        CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF
75→        CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official -> MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_KTLINT_OFFICIAL_CODE_STYLE
76→    }
